source.title: Skyscraper Setup Guide (Full Defense Walkthrough, All Sites)
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions (ASR) — expect garbled operator names and jargon. Only bracketed [MM:SS] markers exist in the source; item timestamps below are anchored to the nearest preceding bracketed marker, not exact utterance times. Creator explicitly frames Skyscraper as one of the most defender-sided maps in the ranked pool, but says it must be set up correctly to hold that advantage. Several operator names are ASR-garbled — see notes on individual items: 'K cause' (likely Kaid, medium confidence), 'Musi' (ambiguous between Melusi and Mozzie), and 'Roma' (not a real operator — almost certainly a mistranscription of 'roamer', a playstyle term, not a name). No attack-side transcript was available for this map. Compass grounding: the official Ubisoft top-down blueprint for this map carries no room-name labels or compass rose, and the transcript itself never uses compass words (only relative terms like 'this side', 'left', 'right'). Per rule 8, no compass prefixes have been added to individual locations — only the two top-floor site clusters could be confidently oriented relative to each other (T/Karaoke/Geisha sits toward the map's west/helipad end, Office/Exhibition/Dragon-Shrine toward the east end), which is noted at the site level, not per-wall.
source.notesCompassGrounding: Attempted: downloaded official Ubisoft blueprint zip (static2.cdn.ubi.com .../r6-maps-skyscraper-blueprints.zip) and inspected all 3 images directly. They show the top floor only, with no text labels and no compass rose, matching the format of Bank's official blueprints. Individual room identities within each site cluster could not be confirmed with certainty from decor alone, so no per-wall compass prefixes were added anywhere in this file.

reinforcements.location: Geisha Wall, Door Side (facing black stairs door)

reinforcements.note: If left soft, an attacker on the door can shoot through it and cut off both exits for whoever is holding that area. Optional, creator's personal preference is against it.

headHoles.location: T/Karaoke Dividing Wall (multiple, all the way across)
headHoles.note: From Karaoke, holds the drum door to T and further into T if the breach opens there. From top black stairs, holds the karaoke window and sees into T. Only make as many as your team will actually use.
Holds a passive angle into the middle of the room from T. Also holds karaoke window from a jump-in-adjacent spot if made.

headHoles.location: Geisha Wall, Stairs Side (toward top black stairs)
headHoles.note: If playing Geisha, holds the cross from top black or the walk-up from top black stairs.

rotates.note: One of the only safe places to hold the black-stairs-side door from. Gives in/out mobility early in the round without using either main door.
Mirrors the black-stairs rotate on the opposite side.
Reinforce later in the round if falling back. Lets you move in/out of Drum without using either main door. Skip feet holes here — a restaurant-stairs push can cut off all of T.

gadgetPlacements.location: Vertical hole in Geisha, over kitchen window
gadgetPlacements.note: Watches attackers running straight through the kitchen window below without droning. Freebie kill.

gadgetPlacements.location: Vert hole in the corner of Karaoke, over bathroom window
gadgetPlacements.note: Attackers often drone the room via a nearby drone hole but skip this vert before jumping the bathroom window below. Freebie angle.
Watches anyone jumping the Geisha window, or anyone walking in once the breach opens. Use a decent SMG or AR here over an SMG-11.

gadgetPlacements.location: Small feet hole near Top Black Stairs

operatorRecommendations.note: Source audio said 'solish', read with high confidence as Solis. Check for a claymore before jumping the Geisha window — commonly placed there. Alternative to a Mute jammer in pocket.

generalTips.note: Only make the head holes that fit your team's plan.
Attack direction on this site is unpredictable compared to other maps' sites.
The karaoke window is a safe spot for attackers post mid-air-ADS removal — expect someone sitting on it, splitting the site in two.
The only safe standing spots in T/Karaoke are tucked behind the two half-walls — watch for verts underneath even there.
Higher-ELO attackers tend to take office first then push across, rather than a direct Geisha/Black Stairs push (more common at lower ELO).

If holding office/this end early, keep at least two defenders present: shoot drones through the drone hole and up house stairs, then give up space slowly as pressure builds. Offer resistance, don't die needlessly.
Check or jam the Geisha window before jumping out — likely claymore most rounds.

reinforcements.note: If left soft and the breach between Office and Exhibition opens, attackers fire straight through into Exhibition. Reinforce both.
Single most important reinforcement on this side of the map. Do not let attackers open this for free at round start — the room becomes very hard to hold otherwise.
Reinforced, paired with the office/mini-bar rotate.
Stops attackers opening it from an angle near the breach, but creates an easy tuck-in plant spot with only two doors left to watch. Not always reinforced.

headHoles.note: Holds the samurai window from here while standing on the table.
Holds anyone jumping the terrace window or coming through the terrace door.

rotates.note: Avoids the Office/Mini Bar door, which can be cut off.
Lets you fight back into the area instead of surrendering it under pressure.
Higher priority than the terrace rotate. Without it, attackers can freely stack in Dragon/Shrine if that wall is reinforced; the rotate contests both sides of the door.

Lets a defender crouch-walk toward the breach without being seen from it (unless attackers are on repel outside). Contests space near the breach; often makes attacker C4 thrown for a table-plant sail over the planter's head harmlessly.

gadgetPlacements.note: Watches anyone pushing up house stairs through the closet/side-entrance door. Free kill if unchecked. Same vert reused when defending Barbecue/Kitchen below.

gadgetPlacements.location: Angle near Samurai Window, tucked from Terrace
gadgetPlacements.note: Popular attacker push spot. Place a proximity-style area-denial gadget here — source audio names the operator phonetically as 'Fenry or Musi'. 'Fenry' read with high confidence as Fenrir. 'Musi' is ambiguous between Melusi and Mozzie — treat as unconfirmed.

Mute jammer, Bandit batteries, or (per garbled audio 'K cause') possibly Kaid electroclaw to stop attackers opening this wall for free early. Mute/Bandit confirmed; the 'Kaid' read is medium-confidence only.
Kaid electroclaw on this window — likely claymore or similar trap there most rounds.

operatorRecommendations.note: Jams the office breach wall to deny a free early-round hard breach.
Electrifies the office breach wall's battery spot alongside/alternative to Mute.
Places a proximity trap near the samurai-window push angle. ('Fenry' in source audio — high confidence.)

generalTips.note: Apart from a direct Geisha/Black-Stairs take, the most important area to hold on the map. Stop the breach wall being opened for free at round start.
The crouch angle attackers commonly hold near the window sits a pixel or two past the barricade tape edge — every barricade shares this.
Prep the run-out window (hit it twice at the bottom) at round start.
Easier to defend than T/Karaoke — attack here is usually a direct push on the breach.
When falling back from Terrace/this area under pressure, don't get cut off from the terrace door and terrace window.
Terrace is not a long-term hold — the door and window both get opened eventually. Use it to absorb a drone or two of utility, then fall back.
Holes along this wall also allow smoke/C4 plays on the breach.

reinforcements.note: Paired with head holes on the right side of pantry watching toward delivery.
Only worth it if playing aggressively in pantry toward delivery — gives a safe haven from being shot out of restaurant. Skip otherwise.
Part of the upstairs hold for this site.
Both this and the Geisha wall are needed since this floor and the floor above are otherwise soft.

headHoles.note: Left soft with head holes instead of a full reinforcement, so attackers can't get free reign up to the side door unchallenged.

rotates.note: Gives a long angle for attackers to see all the way through if made.
Stops attackers holding the single connecting door and cutting the site room in half. Attackers on repel at the nearby window can hold a tight angle across the site through it.
Two reinforcements on this wall for in/out access without the main door — crouch through it (don't stand) for cover behind the bar. No vert to worry about above.

hatches.note: Open, not reinforced, so a pressured defender upstairs can drop down for a last-minute exit.

gadgetPlacements.note: Holds the kitchen window from above.

gadgetPlacements.location: Vert over the popular corner plant spot
gadgetPlacements.note: Watches a very popular plant spot in this corner along the reinforced line.

gadgetPlacements.location: Vert over the site door window
gadgetPlacements.note: Sees both the site door and the rotate made at bottom black stairs from above.

generalTips.note: Several optional walls (e.g. into the bathroom) are a judgment call rather than a fixed recommendation.

Can't defend below, above, and the far side of the map at once — pick priorities. Draw a line across the map at the wall going across drum, cede anything beyond it, and barricade (don't reinforce) the drum door and the door toward shrine.
Watch for attackers repelling at the window near drum and holding a head-glitch angle on the door back to site — quick-peek before running back through.
This floor's ceiling is soft almost everywhere — hold upstairs (Geisha/Drum above) or you'll likely lose the site once attackers get vert.

Common attacker vertical play: gain vert above, use an area-denial gadget (e.g. Ying/Zofia-type) to force the anchor off the site-door window angle, then drop and plant in the popular corner spot. Defend the vert to counter it.
Creator's favourite site on the map — hectic but more predictable to defend than T/Karaoke since attackers usually push this breach directly.

reinforcements.note: Stops attackers opening the window there and shooting straight through the site.
Paired with head holes on the right side. If both walls here are left soft, attackers get free reign up to this door.
Same reasoning as the equivalent wall on the Barbecue/Kitchen site.
Mirrors the Office/Exhibition setup on the other site.

headHoles.note: Left soft with head holes instead of a full reinforcement — gives defenders an angle while still forcing attackers through a watched doorway.
Same head holes as made for the Office/Exhibition site.
Same head holes as made for the Office/Exhibition site.

rotates.note: Gives a couple of different angles through the door instead of relying on one. Preferred over reinforcing the equivalent wall, though reinforcing instead is also reasonable.
The same rotate made for the Office/Exhibition site, reused since the upstairs layout is shared.
Extra rotate between here and mini bar — holds anyone walking in the breach from here, or falls back to play mini bar.

hatches.note: Kept open as a last-minute exit if you hear a plant below and can't see it from above. You'll be seen dropping through the nearby window; the hatch's corner position makes it hard to get a useful angle going down.

gadgetPlacements.note: Same vert used on the Office/Exhibition site, watching anyone pushing up house stairs through the closet door.
Kaid electroclaw here — Ace commonly throws S.E.L.M.A. charges on this wall, so electrifying it denies the hard breach.
Warden with a deployable shield across the opening, combined with the head holes.
Proximity alarm / early-warning gadget on this wall so a defender playing off it gets notice when attackers walk in.

operatorRecommendations.note: Electrifies the closet/side-entrance wall to deny Ace's S.E.L.M.A. charges commonly thrown there.
Plays behind a deployable shield combined with the head holes on this wall.

generalTips.note: Worst site on the map — the other three are far superior. Playing it occasionally can work in your favour since attackers won't know how to approach it.
Hold upstairs on this site (same as Office/Exhibition below it) — the floor is soft almost everywhere, giving attackers fast, easy vertical access.
As with Barbecue/Kitchen, you can't defend below, above, and the far side simultaneously — draw a line and cede the far side.
Delivery is a dangerous spot to sit — the wall/window there will get opened and you can get cut off from another window too, with no great escape route if pushed.
Don't peek outside carelessly from delivery.
You can hide in the bath, though it's much less commonly used here than on Chalet or Villa.
